case_title: SYED M ALI VS. MOHAMMED BHUIYA et al

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DISQUALIFY COURTNEY COATES AND LAW FIRM OF COURTNEY COATES FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALI, SYED M AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MOHAMMED BHUIYA'S MOTION OF TO DISQUALIFY COURTNEY COATES AND THE LAW FIRM OF COURTNEY COATES. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE PARTNERSHIP IS ALLEGED TO HAVE DISSOLVED IN 2005, THE FORMER PARTNERS HAD CONTINUING DEBTS TO ADDRESS AS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH 13 OF THE COMPLAINT IN THIS CASE. IN NEGOTIATING THE WRIGHT SETTLEMENT, MR. COATES NECESSARILY WAS REPRESENTING THOSE INTERESTS AS WELL AS THE INDIVIDUAL INTERESTS OF THE PARTNERS BECAUSE REPRESENTATION OF A PARTNERSHIP IS REPRESENTATION OF EACH PARTNER. WORTHAM & VAN LIEW V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 188 CAL.APP. 3D 927, 932. THE LAW PRESUMES COUNSEL ACQUIRES CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WHEN A PRIOR MATTER HAS A SUBSTANTIAL RELATIONSHIP TO THE CURRENT MATTER AND DISQUALIFICATION IS REQUIRED. FLATT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 9 CAL.4TH 275, 283: HENRIKSEN V.GREAT AMERICAN SAVINGS & LOAN (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 109, 114. HERE, THE CONNECTION EXISTS AND DISQUALIFICATION IS REQUIRED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft Mohammed Bhuiya To Disqualify Courtney Coates And The Lawfirm Of Courtney Coates.
Off Calendar Jun-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MOHAMMED BHUIYA'S Motion Of To Disqualify Courtney Coates And The Law Firm Of Courtney Coates.
APPEARANCES: MARK HOOSHMAND, ESQUIRE (415.863.2345) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: granted. Although the partnership is alleged to have dissolved in 2005, the former partners had continuing debts to address as alleged in paragraph 13 of the complaint in this case. In negotiating the Wright settlement, Mr. Coates necessarily was representing those interests as well as the individual interests of the partners because representation of a partnership is representation of each partner. Wortham & Van Liew v. Superior Court (1987) 188 Cal.App. 3d 927, 932.
The law presumes counsel acquires confidential information when a prior matter has a substantial relationship to the current matter and disqualification is required. Flatt v. Superior Court (1994) 9 Cal.4th 275, 283: Henriksen v.Great American Savings & Loan (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 109, 114. Here, the connection exists and disqualification is required. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
